headline: Does the decline in reading and the rise of screens signal the end of the democratic era?
subtitle: While concerns about the weakening of the mind in the face of new technologies are nothing new, the digital revolution and artificial intelligence now point to a possible end of book civilization.

headline: Russia's shadow looms behind several sabotage and espionage attempts on French soil

In Poland, Jordan Bardella dreams of leading an alliance between Europe's right-wing and far-right parties
subtitle: During a visit to Warsaw and the Belarusian border on June 18 and 19, the president of the Rassemblement National accelerated efforts to build an alliance with European conservatives and liberals – going so far as to distance himself from the Eurosceptic and sovereigntist stance Marine Le Pen has promoted for two decades.

headline: 2026 World Cup: Jérôme Meary, the Frenchman who became an essential football intermediary in the US
subtitle: In just 15 years, the Haute-Savoie native has established himself as a key figure in Major League Soccer, the North American football league. Although nothing initially pointed him toward a career as an agent, a twist of fate changed everything.

headline: Fête de la Musique: Our picks for concerts not to miss
subtitle: Le Monde's Culture desk offers its selection of concerts taking place on Sunday, June 21, as part of the national Fête de la Musique, which has become a global phenomenon.

headline: Thomas Piketty: 'To clarify its own stance and unite its ranks, the left must propose a referendum to adopt a national solidarity tax'
subtitle: As the 2027 presidential campaign draws nearer in France, left-wing parties should take inspiration from a 1945 bill and propose a referendum on imposing a more stringent tax on the wealthy, writes economist Thomas Piketty. He believes up to €800 billion could be raised.

'They call us misfits, degenerates': Free parties fall under French government scrutiny
subtitle: Thousands of people gathered on June 6 in the south of France for a rave party, despite the events being banned locally until the end of the year. Participants defend them as among the last spaces of freedom outside of commercial and institutional frameworks.

headline: In Trump's polarized America, empathy faces unanimous opposition
subtitle: Once valued in American public life, empathy now faces criticism from the far right, which sees it as a sign of weakness. The left, too, is questioning its relevance: In a world where brutality prevails, can we still afford to show understanding?

headline: French security replaces Palantir with domestic firm ChapsVision, a complicated choice
subtitle: Used since the 2015 terrorist attacks, the controversial American company's data analysis software is set to be gradually replaced by that of a French provider. The long-awaited decision presents a significant technological challenge.
